question:
Solve the following arithmetic problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

Trevor is currently 11 years old and his older brother is 20 years old. How old will Trevor be when his older brother is three times as old as Trevor is now?
response:
Okay, so Trevor is 11 now, and his brother is 20. I think the question wants us to figure out how old Trevor will be when his brother is three times Trevor’s current age.  

Trevor’s current age is 11. Three times that is 33. So his brother will be 33 years old when this happens. But wait, his brother is already 20, so how many years until he’s 33? Um... I think it’s 33 minus 20, which is 13 years.  

If it takes 13 years, then Trevor will also age 13 more years. So 11 plus 13 is... 24? I think Trevor will be 24 when this happens. I hope I did that right!
